scene_hint_profiles.hint_text: Start from the north-shore gate, then turn toward the lake core.
Look at the near structure first, then compare it with the surrounding scene.
Give one next stop with a reason, instead of listing many choices.

hot_questions_by_spot.question_cluster: axial relation with lake and mountain
hot_questions_by_spot.answer_strategy: Start from spatial position, then explain why it works as the visual opening point of the whole complex.

hot_questions_by_spot.question_cluster: why it feels like the center of the whole waterscape
hot_questions_by_spot.answer_strategy: Explain it through bridge-water-pavilion composition before going into history.

hot_questions_by_spot.question_cluster: what is special about the yunshi in the boat-house scene
hot_questions_by_spot.answer_strategy: Describe the visual texture and symbolic role of the stone, then connect it back to the waterside setting.

hot_questions_by_spot.question_cluster: which stele should be looked at first
hot_questions_by_spot.answer_strategy: Do not list every stele at once. Give one entry point and then suggest a second comparison target.

common_confusions.symptom: The visitor stays on the overview for a while and does not know where to begin.
common_confusions.recommended_hint: Offer a single calm entry route and recommend starting with weiyuan-temple.

common_confusions.symptom: The visitor reads the temple as one building and misses its relation to the lake, the mountain, and the entry sequence.
common_confusions.recommended_hint: Reconnect the temple to the north-shore gate and the lake-facing opening before giving detailed facts.

common_confusions.symptom: The visitor sees many tablets at once and cannot tell where to look first.
common_confusions.recommended_hint: Offer one first stele and one comparison stele instead of a long catalog.

route_acceptance_patterns.best_intro_style: soft opening with one clear first stop and a reason tied to the whole-site composition
invite the visitor to follow the water rhythm and let the bridge-pavilion sequence lead the scene

blind_spots.overlooked_target: its role between mountain and lake, not only the front hall itself
blind_spots.best_recovery_hint: Ask the visitor to imagine the north-shore approach and then read the temple as a hinge in the whole composition.

blind_spots.overlooked_target: the yunshi detail and how it changes the boat-house atmosphere
blind_spots.best_recovery_hint: Pull attention from the facade inward to the stone detail and then back out to the waterside mood.

blind_spots.overlooked_target: the bridge-water-pavilion sequence as one scene rather than three separate objects
blind_spots.best_recovery_hint: Guide the eye from bridge to pavilion to reflection, then explain the historical layer.
